blocks.content: Things Money Can't Buy (1)
Black Magician.
A research group formed by six problem students led by Jake.
Except for Jake, it was a group of students who repeatedly failed to advance grades, with no dreams or future.
They too once had a time when they were recognized as brilliant talents. If they had the courage to try again, they might have achieved victory someday.
But they gave up on competition themselves. In a way, they were the only dropouts in the midst of harsh competition.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: This can't be. Something's wrong.
Jake shook his head.
It was impossible for Black Magician to lose to a freshman who just entered, though it might be understandable with Amy.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Right. We didn't lose. We just had bad luck.
The students Jake had forced to drop out until now had much higher grades than Shirone.
In the end, it was just bad luck. Doesn't everyone have such days from time to time in life?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not over yet. I'm Class Four. I'm fundamentally different from those who just strike from behind with anti-magic.
Shirone tensed up as he read Jake's killing intent.
Jake's spirit zone, which had changed into a star shape, was felt through synesthesia. It felt as if hundreds of archers were aiming their bows.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What should I do? I haven't learned magic yet.
While anti-magic was a battle of spirit zones that could be countered, it was a different story when it came to magical combat.
There was no way Shirone, who hadn't even mastered the basic defensive magic of Air Shield, let alone attack magic, could face Jake.
Jake created wind between his palms and compressed it as if squeezing it.
Then the air began to rotate in the form of a disc.
It was Wind Cutter, which could easily cut through logs.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: I'll take your arm first. Then your legs.
Just as Jake was about to cast Wind Cutter, someone grabbed his shoulder from behind and turned him around.
At the same moment he sensed the approach through synesthesia, he was touched.
It wasn't human speed.
He saw Amy's face with her red eyes raised.
Her fist struck his chin, and the scenery before his eyes shook.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Ugh!
With the power of schema in a physical attack against a magician who was weak against physical attacks, there was no way his spirit zone could withstand it.
Amy straddled Jake's stomach and repeatedly threw punches.
Each blow felt like being hit with a rock.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What are you going to do to me? Say it again, you bastard! I said say it again!
Jake's face shook left and right as if it was about to be pulled off.
Thinking that something terrible would happen if this continued, Shirone ran to Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Senior, stop! He'll die!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Let go, you! You underestimated me!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm telling you he's going to die!
Jake heard the conversation through his fading consciousness.
He didn't feel pain, only dull impacts coming in.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Die? I'm going to die?
The fear of death awakened Jake's mind.
He covered his face with both arms and shouted at the top of his lungs.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: S-save me! Just spare my life! Please!
Amy suddenly felt tears welling up.
She had almost lost an eye.
The thought that she had been humiliated by such a piece of trash made her blood boil.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I'll never forgive you! You bastard!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Senior, please stop!
Shirone pulled Amy with all his might.
Her schema's body balance was so strong that it felt like pulling out a stake stuck in the ground.
With all his strength, he pulled, and finally Amy fell backwards, crushing Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's enough, Senior. Please stop.
Shirone exhaled roughly.
When he came to his senses, he felt something soft and squishy against his arm.
Realizing that he was pressing against Amy's chest, he hastily removed his hand.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: S-sorry!
Amy showed no reaction.
She was just breathing heavily and trembling intermittently.
As her emotions were transmitted through his body, Shirone felt a sense of pity.
He gently embraced her waist and spoke softly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Senior, because of me...

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ah! That feels good~!
Amy bounced up, striking Shirone's lower abdomen with her buttocks.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Oof!
Shirone clutched his stomach and rolled over.
If he had been hit a little lower, a terrible situation would have occurred.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What's wrong? Are you feeling unwell?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, nothing.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hehe! Of course. How light am I?
Having been curious from a young age, she couldn't not know Shirone's condition.
It was her own way of expressing intimacy for someone who was awkward with emotional expression.
It was also her way of overlooking the unintentional intimate contact Shirone had succeeded in making.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Anyway, what do we do now?
Shirone changed the subject and turned his attention to Black Magician.
He hadn't realized it during the chaotic fight, but seeing the trouble they'd caused, their condition was serious.
Every one of them was unconscious with nosebleeds, and Jake was crawling on the ground in a half-deranged state.
After examining their appearance, Amy decided to leave it at that.
As much as she wanted to get them expelled, if the incident grew bigger, Shirone's identity might be revealed.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Let's go down. Those guys will stay quiet from now on since they have something to be ashamed of.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Senior Seriel will be coming this way, won't she?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What? Don't tell me you told the teacher?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course I did. How could I come alone when you were in danger?
Amy sighed and held her forehead.
Though she had been humiliated, she had thought she could at least reap the rewards of victory.
But hearing that the report was already finished made her deflated.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Senior, thank you.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Huh?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You came alone because of me, right? But don't worry. I don't care about secrets anymore. I'm just glad you're safe.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No, I'm not...
Amy scratched her cheek and avoided his gaze.
In the atmosphere of the Karmis family, which symbolized free-spiritedness, she had once felt neglected.
She longed for human warmth and wandered the back alleys.
For her, Shirone's kind words were pleasant but of a nature that she couldn't fully digest.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmph! Who said it was because of you? I can handle guys like these on my own. Actually, I've been wanting to do this for a while. They just happened to give me the perfect chance.
Amy's cheeks were completely red.
Shirone was satisfied with that.
He felt like he understood what kind of person she was.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hehehe, I can't stand how childish this is. You call yourselves geniuses? No way. You're just kids who don't know how the world works.
Jake stood up, leaning his hand against a tree.
His face was in tatters, but his eyes were full of venom.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You really are pathetic. If you can't even stand up, just stay down. Are you some kind of pervert who enjoys getting beaten by me?

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Hahahahaha!
There was not a thought in Jake's mind that he had lost.
In the end, money rules the world.
He might have lost the fight, but he still had powerful financial resources.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Think it's all over? No, it's just beginning. Let me be honest. You can't really kill me, can you? Go ahead and try if you're confident. As long as I'm in school, you'll never graduate. I'll ruin you by any means necessary!
Shirone was horrified.
But Amy, who had controlled the back alleys since childhood, knew people like Jake all too well.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Kill you? Why would I kill you? My life is too precious to waste on someone like you. But know this. I may not be able to kill you, but I can beat you up.
Jake flinched as Amy approached with her fist clenched.
No matter how determined one's mind is, the body remembers fear.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: ...You think I'm scared?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Who said that? Since you're up, you need another beating. I'm still not satisfied.
As Amy ostentatiously rotated her shoulders, Jake was furious, but his face was contorted with fear.
Keeeeeeee!
A sharp friction sound swept in from the sky.
Shirone looked up, but the other two knew without looking.
It was the distinctive noise of Spatial Movement, an advanced magic of teleportation.
Just as he thought a starlight was twinkling in the sky, the flash curved rapidly and descended to where Shirone and his companions were.
Seriel landed with two teachers.
Amy checked the teachers with the mindset of scratching a lottery ticket.
Then she let out a small sigh.
Siina of ice magic and Saad of flame magic.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Sigh, of all people.
There was no way they, along with Etella, the youngest teaching staff at the magic school, would handle this incident lightly.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Amy! Are you okay? Are you hurt anywhere?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yes, I'm fine. There's no way I'd be taken down easily.
Seriel ran to Amy with a teary face.
Seriel, with her delicate personality, didn't miss the small changes.
Amy's bangs, which slightly covered her right eye, were singed.
She had always thought Amy's hairstyle was too retro compared to her pretty appearance, but seeing her friend's hair that she had maintained for six years damaged made her heart ache.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: What happened to your hair? What happened?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Oh come on! It's nothing. By the way, is it past 7 o'clock already?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: What are you talking about? Do you know how long we searched for you? If it weren't for the teachers, we wouldn't have arrived in time.
The sun had already set, and darkness was falling.
As it became pitch black, Saad cast a flame magic.
A fireball that burst forth with a 'poof' burned the void like a small sun.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So this is Fire Sun magic.
Despite the surroundings brightening as if it were daylight, no heat was felt.
This was a phenomenon impossible without converting thermal energy into light energy.
According to what he remembered from books, Fire Sun was a high-level technique despite its appearance and was mainly seen on battlefields.
Saad approached Amy with a sad face.

blocks.speaker: Saad
blocks.content: What is this all about, Amy! How could someone as outstanding as you do such a foolish thing...
Saad, who was only weak to women, seemed to have no interest in Shirone.
Especially with Amy, who had the top grades, was from a first-class family, and had the best looks, he couldn't help but cherish her.
Siina, who felt disgusted by Saad's attitude, turned to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Shirone, can you explain what happened?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Uh, well, that...

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: I'll explain that.
The teachers turned their heads at Jake's words.
It was suspicious that the perpetrator of the incident would offer to explain.
They wondered if he had gone crazy from being beaten too much.
But this too was Jake's calculation.
He could take the punishment if need be.
The school, which needed the Ardius family's financial power, would never expel him anyway.
But what about Shirone?
He had clearly heard him talk about secrets in their earlier conversation.
Plus, Amy's behavior of trying to cover it up.
In the end, it meant they had something to hide.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: I called out Amy. Because her skills were superior, I had no choice but to use anti-magic. But it was a fair match. It was a pride battle that escalated after deepening emotional differences over time. As you can see, our side is the one that's been severely beaten.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Are you asking me to believe that? You've violated school rules.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: I'm not saying we did well. But think about it. If Amy was the victim, why didn't she report it?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What nonsense! What did we hide? You threatened that you had kidnapped Shirone!

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Haha! Did we actually kidnap him? No. Moreover, Shirone read our note but didn't tell the teacher. What does this imply?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You, you bastard...
Amy couldn't refute.
For someone unaware of the circumstances, it was incomprehensible that Shirone, who wasn't kidnapped, rushed over without reporting to a teacher.
But telling the truth would reveal Shirone's secret.
